step.title: Discovery and Context
step.goal: Understand what command to create, what it should do, and how users will invoke it.

substep.title: Gather Requirements
substep.ask:
Let's create a new command. Tell me:
1. **What should this command do?** (One sentence describing its purpose)
2. **What triggers it?** (What user action or need leads to using this command?)
3. **Does it take arguments?** (What input might users provide after /command-name?)

substep.title: Check for Related Artifacts
substep.action: Search for any related documentation: - Is there a skill this should wrap? - Are there existing similar commands? - Is there architecture documentation?

check.output: Found related skill: {{skill_name}} This command might be a wrapper that delegates to this skill.

substep.title: Summarize Requirements

section.title: Good Command Candidates

rules.rule: Frequently repeated actions that need a shortcut
Operations that benefit from slash-trigger invocation
Wrappers around complex skills for quick access
Agent-specific utilities that shouldn't be global

section.example: "/commit" - Frequently needed, benefits from quick access "/pr-review" - Wraps complex skill for easy invocation "/prompter/verify" - Agent-specific utility

section.title: When NOT to Create a Command

rules.rule: If it needs multi-step user interaction → create a skill instead
If it requires checkpoints and review → create a skill instead
If it's complex enough to need step files → create a skill instead

section.example: "Create a PRD" → Skill (needs checkpoints) "Commit my changes" → Command (quick action) "Validate this file" → Could be either (depends on complexity)
